name: Banking Vertical Subpack
description: Vertical-specialized value intelligence for Banking sub-segments: Retail, Commercial, Investment, Community/Regional, Credit Unions, Digital Banks, Private Banking, Treasury, Payments/Cards, and Lending (Mortgage/Auto/SMB). Provides banking-specific pains, KPIs, formulas, benchmarks, signal rules, personas, discovery questions, objections, technology systems, regulatory factors, worked examples, and buying triggers.

pains.name: NIM Compression from Deposit Beta >70%
pains.description: Deposit beta exceeding 70% indicates that deposit pricing is highly rate-sensitive, compressing NIM faster than loan yields can offset. Community and regional banks are particularly exposed due to reliance on interest-bearing deposits.
symptoms: cost of funds increasing >20bps per quarter
sources: S&P Global Market Intelligence Bank Scorecards

pains.name: Mortgage Origination Cost Exceeding $12,000 per Loan
pains.description: Manual document collection, fragmented LOS ecosystems, appraisal delays, and compliance checks extending time-to-close while unit costs remain elevated above industry benchmarks.
sources: ICE Mortgage Technology Origination Insight Report

pains.name: Branch Network Operating Cost Inflation
pains.description: Legacy branch networks with declining foot traffic absorbing >30% of non-interest expense while digital transaction share exceeds 85%. Branch rationalization delayed by community commitments and lease obligations.
symptoms: branch FTE cost >50% of retail ops
lease renewal decisions pending >20% of network
sources: J.D. Power Retail Banking Satisfaction Study 2024

pains.name: Commercial Real Estate (CRE) Concentration and Refinancing Cliff
pains.description: Office and multifamily CRE loans maturing into a higher-rate environment with declining property valuations. CRE concentrations >300% of capital creating regulatory scrutiny and provisioning pressure.
sources: Federal Reserve Senior Loan Officer Opinion Survey

pains.name: Auto Lending Lease-End and Residual Value Risk
pains.description: Used vehicle price decline eroding residual value assumptions on leased vehicles. Lease-end volume surging while remarketing channels absorb capacity. Subprime auto delinquencies rising.
symptoms: lease return volume >20% of auto portfolio

pains.name: Digital Account Opening Abandonment and Identity Verification Friction
pains.description: Digital onboarding abandonment >60% due to identity verification document requests, manual review queues, and KYC name-matching failures. Neobank competitors offering <3-minute account opening.

pains.name: Treasury Management Client Deposit Beta Erosion
pains.description: Commercial clients migrating operating deposits to money market funds and TreasuryDirect as ECR (earnings credit rate) fails to keep pace with market rates. Treasury services non-interest revenue declining.

pains.name: Card Interchange Revenue Compression and Rewards Cost Inflation
pains.description: Durbin Amendment extension to credit cards under legislative consideration, combined with premium rewards program costs rising faster than interchange yield. CAC via card acquisition becoming uneconomical.

pains.name: Commercial Lending RFP Win Rate Decline
pains.description: Corporate and middle-market clients demanding faster credit decisions, digital self-service, and integrated treasury solutions. Traditional banks losing RFPs to regional competitors and direct lenders.

pains.name: Private Banking Client Acquisition and Succession Crisis
pains.description: Aging advisor workforce with average age >55 and insufficient digital tools for next-gen client engagement. Client assets at risk as inheriting generation prefers digital-first wealth management.

pains.name: Core Banking System API Limitations and Vendor Lock-in
pains.description: Legacy core banking systems lacking real-time APIs preventing digital product innovation. Vendor contract renewal terms increasing >8% annually with declining service levels.

pains.name: Community Bank Efficiency Ratio Deterioration >65%
pains.description: Non-interest expense growing faster than revenue in community and regional banks. Technology spend constrained by asset size, yet regulatory compliance costs fixed. Scale disadvantage intensifying.

pains.name: Real-Time Payment Fraud Detection Latency Gap
pains.description: FedNow and RTP rails reducing settlement time to seconds, but fraud detection systems operating in batch or with >300ms latency. APP scams exploiting irrevocable settlement.
symptoms: APP scam losses >0.2% of RTP volume

pains.name: Small Business Deposit and Lending Relationship Fragmentation
pains.description: SMB primary bank relationships fragmenting as fintechs capture deposit, payment, and lending share. SMB clients using 3+ financial providers reducing deposit stickiness and cross-sell opportunity.
sources: J.D. Power Small Business Banking Study

pains.name: Credit Card Portfolio Yield Compression and Charge-off Creep
pains.description: Credit card APRs capped by competitive pressure and regulatory scrutiny while charge-off rates rising in subprime segments. Portfolio yield compression threatening revolving credit profitability.
symptoms: cost of funds on card funding >5%
sources: Federal Reserve G.19 Consumer Credit Report
S&P Global Credit Card ABS Performance

pains.name: Investment Banking Deal Pipeline Compression and Fee Pressure
pains.description: M&A and IPO activity down >30% from peak with fee compression on advisory and underwriting. Boutique and regional competitors underpricing legacy bulge-bracket relationships.

pains.name: Digital Bank Unit Economics and Path-to-Profitability Failure
pains.description: Neobanks and digital banks burning cash with CAC exceeding first-year revenue, deposit acquisition costs >$150 per account, and interchange yield insufficient to cover operating losses.
sources: CB Insights State of Fintech Q2 2024

pains.name: Credit Union Membership Growth Stagnation and Demographic Aging
pains.description: Credit union membership growth <2% with average member age >50. Failed to attract Gen Z/millennial members. Technology investment lagging community banks. Field of membership constraints limiting expansion.
symptoms: young member acquisition <10% of new members

kpis.name: Deposit Beta
kpis.formula: (Change in Cost of Deposits / Change in Federal Funds Rate) * 100

kpis.name: Net Interest Margin (NIM)
kpis.formula: ((Interest Income - Interest Expense) / Average Earning Assets) * 100

kpis.name: Cost of Funds
kpis.formula: Total Interest Expense / Average Interest-Bearing Liabilities

kpis.name: Mortgage Cost-to-Close
kpis.formula: Total Origination Cost / Closed Loan Volume (count)

kpis.name: Mortgage Cycle Time (Days to Close)
kpis.formula: Average Days from Application to Funding

kpis.name: Mortgage Pull-Through Rate
kpis.formula: (Funded Loans / Approved Applications) * 100

kpis.name: Branch Cost per Transaction
kpis.formula: Total Branch Operating Cost / Total Branch Transactions

kpis.name: Digital Transaction Share
kpis.formula: (Digital Transactions / Total Transactions) * 100

kpis.name: Branch FTE Cost Ratio
kpis.formula: (Branch FTE Cost / Total Retail Operating Cost) * 100

kpis.name: CRE Concentration Ratio
kpis.formula: (CRE Loans / Risk-Based Capital) * 100

kpis.name: CRE Watch List Ratio
kpis.formula: (CRE Watch List Loans / Total CRE Loans) * 100

kpis.name: Office Vacancy Rate (Portfolio)
kpis.formula: (Vacant Office Square Feet / Total Office Square Feet in Portfolio) * 100

kpis.name: Auto Lease Residual Loss per Unit
kpis.formula: (Actual Residual Value - Projected Residual Value) per Unit

kpis.name: Auto Delinquency Rate (60+ DPD)
kpis.formula: (Auto Loans 60+ DPD / Total Auto Loans) * 100

kpis.name: Auto Remarketing Days-to-Sell
kpis.formula: Average Days from Lease End to Sale

kpis.name: Digital Account Opening Abandonment Rate
kpis.formula: (Abandoned Applications / Total Applications Started) * 100

kpis.name: Identity Verification Failure Rate
kpis.formula: (Failed IDV Attempts / Total IDV Attempts) * 100

kpis.name: Digital Onboarding NPS
kpis.formula: Net Promoter Score for Digital Onboarding Experience

kpis.name: Treasury Client Deposit Beta
kpis.formula: (Change in Commercial Deposit Cost / Change in Market Rate) * 100

kpis.name: ECR Spread to Fed Funds
kpis.formula: ECR Rate - Effective Federal Funds Rate

kpis.name: Treasury Client RFP Win Rate
kpis.formula: (Won RFPs / Total RFPs Submitted) * 100

kpis.name: Card Interchange Yield
kpis.formula: (Interchange Revenue / Card Purchase Volume) * 10000

kpis.name: Rewards Cost as % of Interchange
kpis.formula: (Rewards Expense / Interchange Revenue) * 100

kpis.name: Card CAC (Customer Acquisition Cost)
kpis.formula: Total Card Marketing & Acquisition Cost / New Card Accounts

kpis.name: Commercial RFP Win Rate
kpis.formula: (Won Commercial RFPs / Total Commercial RFPs) * 100

kpis.name: Commercial Credit Decision Time
kpis.formula: Average Days from Application to Credit Decision

kpis.name: Commercial Cross-Sell Ratio
kpis.formula: Average Number of Products per Commercial Client

kpis.name: Private Banking AUM per Advisor
kpis.formula: Total Private Banking AUM / Number of Private Banking Advisors

kpis.name: Next-Gen Client Engagement Rate
kpis.formula: (Next-Gen Clients with Active Digital Engagement / Total Next-Gen Clients) * 100

kpis.name: Advisor Attrition Rate
kpis.formula: (Advisors Lost / Average Advisor Count) * 100

kpis.name: Core System API Availability
kpis.formula: (API Uptime Minutes / Total Calendar Minutes) * 100

kpis.name: Product Time-to-Market
kpis.formula: Days from Concept to Production Launch

kpis.name: Core Vendor Contract Inflation Rate
kpis.formula: ((Current Contract Value - Prior Contract Value) / Prior Contract Value) * 100

kpis.name: Efficiency Ratio
kpis.formula: (Non-Interest Expense / (Net Interest Income + Non-Interest Income)) * 100

kpis.name: Revenue per Employee
kpis.formula: Total Revenue / Total FTE Count

kpis.name: Compliance Cost Ratio
kpis.formula: (Total Compliance Cost / Non-Interest Expense) * 100

kpis.name: Real-Time Fraud Detection Latency
kpis.formula: Average Milliseconds from Transaction to Decline Decision

kpis.name: APP Scam Loss Rate
kpis.formula: (APP Scam Losses / RTP Transaction Volume) * 100

kpis.name: Fraud False Positive Rate (Real-Time)
kpis.formula: (False Positive Declines / Total Declines) * 100

kpis.name: SMB Primary Bank Share
kpis.formula: (SMB Clients Naming Bank as Primary / Total SMB Clients) * 100

kpis.name: SMB Deposit Growth Rate
kpis.formula: ((Current SMB Deposits - Prior SMB Deposits) / Prior SMB Deposits) * 100

kpis.name: SMB Loan Application Abandonment Rate
kpis.formula: (Abandoned SMB Loan Applications / Total Started) * 100

kpis.name: Credit Card Portfolio Yield
kpis.formula: (Interest + Fee Revenue) / Average Receivables * 100

kpis.name: Credit Card Charge-Off Rate
kpis.formula: (Charge-Offs / Average Receivables) * 100

kpis.name: Credit Card 30+ Delinquency Rate
kpis.formula: (Receivables 30+ DPD / Total Receivables) * 100

kpis.name: Investment Banking Advisory Revenue per MD
kpis.formula: Total Advisory Revenue / Managing Director Count

kpis.name: Pitch-to-Win Ratio
kpis.formula: (Won Mandates / Total Pitches) * 100

kpis.name: Underwriting Fee Margin
kpis.formula: (Underwriting Revenue / Capital Raised) * 100

kpis.name: Digital Bank CAC
kpis.formula: Total Marketing & Acquisition Cost / New Accounts

kpis.name: Deposit Balance per Account
kpis.formula: Total Deposits / Number of Deposit Accounts

kpis.name: Monthly Burn Rate per Account
kpis.formula: Monthly Operating Loss / Number of Active Accounts

kpis.name: Credit Union Membership Growth Rate
kpis.formula: ((Current Members - Prior Members) / Prior Members) * 100

kpis.name: Average Member Age
kpis.formula: Average Age of Credit Union Members

kpis.name: Digital Engagement Rate (Credit Union)
kpis.formula: (Members with Digital Login in Last 30 Days / Total Members) * 100

valueDrivers.signalPattern: Deposit beta >70% with NIM declining >15bps YoY
Brokered deposit dependence >10% with cost of funds rising >20bps quarterly
Cost-to-close >$8,000 with cycle time >45 days
Pull-through rate <65% with rework rate >15%
Branch cost per transaction >$6 with digital transaction share >85%
Branch FTE cost ratio >50% with lease renewals pending >20% of network
CRE concentration >300% of capital with watch list >8%
Office vacancy >20% in portfolio with refinancing pipeline >$500M maturing
Residual value losses >$500 per unit with auto delinquency >3.5%
Remarketing days-to-sell >45 with subprime charge-off rate >8%
Digital onboarding abandonment >60% with IDV failure rate >25%
Manual review queue >48 hours with KYC false reject rate >15%
Operating deposit outflows >8% quarterly with ECR spread <50bps
Treasury RFP loss rate >25% with client NPS <30
Interchange yield declining >5bps annually with rewards cost >45% of interchange
Card CAC >$300 with active card rate <65%
Commercial RFP win rate <30% with credit decision time >14 days
Cross-sell ratio <2.5 products with relationship manager turnover >15%
Average advisor age >55 with AUM per advisor <$100M
Next-gen engagement rate <20% with client attrition >10% post-retirement
API response time >500ms with batch windows >4 hours
Vendor contract renewal >$50M with product launch cycle >12 months
Efficiency ratio >65% with compliance cost >8% of non-interest expense
Technology spend <3% of revenue with revenue per employee <$350K
Fraud detection latency >300ms on RTP/FedNow rails
APP scam losses >0.2% of RTP volume with reimbursement rate >40%
SMB primary bank share <40% with deposit outflows >10% annually
SMB loan abandonment >55% with cross-sell <2 products
Portfolio yield <18% with charge-off rate >4.5%
30+ delinquency >3% with promotional balance share >25%
Advisory revenue decline >20% with MD compensation as % of revenue >35%
Pitch-to-win ratio <15% with underwriting fees <2.5%
CAC >$200 per account with monthly burn >$10M
Interchange revenue per account <$15/month with deposit balance <$1,500
Membership growth <2% with average member age >50
Digital engagement <30% with technology spend <2.5% of assets

formulas.name: Deposit Beta Improvement NIM Value
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with ALCO model access; MEDIUM with peer benchmark; LOW in extreme rate volatility

formulas.name: Mortgage Cost-to-Close and Cycle Time Reduction
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with LOS data; MEDIUM using industry average cost; LOW for non-delegated correspondent channels

formulas.name: Branch Network Rationalization Savings
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with branch P&L data; MEDIUM using industry branch cost benchmark; LOW without lease exit strategy

formulas.name: CRE Risk Mitigation and Provision Avoidance
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with loan-level data; MEDIUM using segment provision rates; LOW during market dislocation

formulas.name: Auto Lease Residual Value Protection
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with lease-level data; MEDIUM using industry residual curves; LOW for new lease products

formulas.name: Digital Onboarding Conversion Improvement
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with funnel data; MEDIUM using industry benchmark; LOW for new digital brands

formulas.name: Treasury Deposit Retention Value
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with treasury client P&L; MEDIUM using industry benchmark; LOW for new treasury products

formulas.name: Card Interchange and Rewards Optimization
formulas.confidenceRules: MEDIUM with portfolio data; LOW during interchange regulation changes; HIGH with 24+ month history

formulas.name: Commercial Banking RFP and Speed Value
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with CRM data; MEDIUM using industry benchmark; LOW for new geographic markets

formulas.name: Private Banking Advisor Productivity and Succession
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with platform usage data; MEDIUM with industry productivity curves; LOW during market downturns

formulas.name: Core Banking Modernization ROI
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with current state costed; MEDIUM using benchmark; LOW for greenfield implementations

formulas.name: Community Bank Efficiency Improvement
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with expense breakdown; MEDIUM using peer benchmark; LOW for institutions <$500M assets

formulas.name: Real-Time Payment Fraud Prevention Value
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with 12+ months RTP fraud data; MEDIUM using industry benchmark; LOW for new RTP adopters

formulas.name: SMB Banking Revenue Recovery
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with SMB cohort data; MEDIUM using SBA benchmark; LOW for new geographic markets

formulas.name: Credit Union Digital Transformation Value
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with member data; MEDIUM using industry benchmark; LOW for credit unions <$100M assets

benchmarks.name: Top Quartile NIM (Banking >$10B)
benchmarks.notes: Top quartile banks >$10B assets. Community banks typically 20-40bps higher.

benchmarks.name: Top Quartile NIM (Community Banks <$10B)
benchmarks.notes: Higher due to relationship pricing and lower cost of funds.

benchmarks.name: Deposit Beta (Low/Optimal)
benchmarks.notes: Banks with strong core deposit franchises. High beta banks >70%.

benchmarks.name: Mortgage Cost-to-Close (Best-in-Class)
benchmarks.notes: Fully loaded cost including origination, processing, underwriting, closing.

benchmarks.name: Mortgage Cycle Time (Best-in-Class)
benchmarks.notes: Purchase loans. Refinance cycle time typically 15-20% shorter.

benchmarks.name: Mortgage Pull-Through Rate (Top Quartile)
benchmarks.notes: Purchase applications. Higher for portfolio lenders than correspondent.

benchmarks.name: Branch Cost per Transaction (Industry Average)
benchmarks.notes: Fully loaded. Digital/mobile transaction cost typically $0.15-0.35.

benchmarks.name: Digital Transaction Share (Top Quartile)
benchmarks.notes: Top quartile national banks. Regional banks typically 75-85%.

benchmarks.name: CRE Concentration (Regulatory Comfort Zone)
benchmarks.notes: Concentrations >300% trigger enhanced scrutiny. >400% considered elevated risk.

benchmarks.name: Office Vacancy Rate (National Average)
benchmarks.notes: Varies significantly by market. CBD Class A may be 10-12% higher.

benchmarks.name: Auto Delinquency Rate (60+ DPD, Prime)
benchmarks.notes: Prime segment. Subprime typically 6-10%.

benchmarks.name: Digital Onboarding Abandonment (Best-in-Class)
benchmarks.notes: Top-performing neobanks achieve <20%. Traditional banks often 50-65%.

benchmarks.name: Identity Verification Pass Rate (Optimal)
benchmarks.notes: Balancing fraud prevention and friction. Too high (>98%) may indicate weak KYC.

benchmarks.name: Treasury RFP Win Rate (Top Quartile)
benchmarks.notes: Relationship banks. Transactional banks typically 30-45%.

benchmarks.name: Card Interchange Yield (Debit Ex-Durbin)
benchmarks.notes: Non-exempt debit typically 40-60bps lower. Credit interchange 180-220bps.

benchmarks.name: Rewards Cost as % of Interchange (Optimal)
benchmarks.notes: Premium cards may reach 45-55%. Optimal depends on portfolio strategy.

benchmarks.name: Commercial Credit Decision Time (Best-in-Class)
benchmarks.notes: Straight-through processing for renewals. New credits typically 7-14 days.

benchmarks.name: Efficiency Ratio (Top Quartile National)
benchmarks.notes: Top quartile national banks. Community banks typically 55-65%.

benchmarks.name: Efficiency Ratio (Top Quartile Community)
benchmarks.notes: Asset-sensitive community banks may exceed 60% in rising rate environments.

benchmarks.name: Credit Card Charge-Off Rate (Prime)
benchmarks.notes: Prime segment. Near-prime 3.5-5.0%. Subprime 6-10%.

benchmarks.name: APP Scam Loss Rate (RTP, Target)
benchmarks.notes: UK Faster Payments experience. US RTP newer, limited data.

benchmarks.name: SMB Primary Bank Share (Target)
benchmarks.notes: Top relationship banks. Average typically 35-45%.

benchmarks.name: Private Banking AUM per Advisor (Top Quartile)
benchmarks.notes: Wirehouse advisors typically higher. Bank-based private banking may be lower.

benchmarks.name: Digital Bank CAC (Best-in-Class)
benchmarks.notes: Organic channels. Paid acquisition typically $150-350. Chime reported ~$50-80.

benchmarks.name: Credit Union Membership Growth (Top Quartile)
benchmarks.notes: Top quartile. National average 2-3%. Digital-first CUs may exceed 10%.

signalRules.signalPattern: Deposit beta >70% in two consecutive quarters
signalRules.recommendedAction: Schedule ALCO deep-dive on deposit repricing strategy and brokered deposit alternatives

signalRules.signalPattern: Cost-to-close >$10,000 or cycle time >50 days for two months
signalRules.recommendedAction: Initiate LOS workflow optimization assessment with vendor and process mining

signalRules.signalPattern: Branch cost per transaction >$7 with digital share >88% and lease renewal pending
signalRules.recommendedAction: Launch branch optimization study with lease exit economics and digital migration plan

signalRules.signalPattern: CRE concentration >300% of RBC OR watch list >8% of CRE portfolio
signalRules.recommendedAction: Engage portfolio management team on CRE concentration reduction strategy and refinancing support

signalRules.signalPattern: Residual loss >$500 per unit for two consecutive quarters
signalRules.recommendedAction: Review residual value assumptions and remarketing channel efficiency; evaluate lease extension program

signalRules.signalPattern: Onboarding abandonment >60% OR IDV failure >25% for three weeks
signalRules.recommendedAction: Launch UX audit of onboarding flow and IDV vendor performance review; A/B test streamlined KYC

signalRules.signalPattern: Commercial operating deposit outflows >8% quarterly OR ECR spread <50bps
signalRules.recommendedAction: Review ECR schedule against competitive benchmarks; launch treasury client retention outreach

signalRules.signalPattern: Interchange yield declining >5bps annually OR rewards cost >50% of interchange
signalRules.recommendedAction: Evaluate rewards program ROI and interchange optimization; assess Durbin 2.0 legislative scenario planning

signalRules.signalPattern: RFP win rate <25% for two quarters OR decision time >18 days
signalRules.recommendedAction: Conduct win/loss analysis on last 20 RFPs; benchmark decision speed against top 3 competitors

signalRules.signalPattern: Average advisor age >55 with AUM per advisor <$100M OR next-gen engagement <15%
signalRules.recommendedAction: Develop next-gen digital platform roadmap and advisor succession planning with recruitment acceleration

signalRules.signalPattern: API response time >500ms OR product launch cycle >14 months OR vendor increase >8%
signalRules.recommendedAction: Initiate core banking modernization business case with API-first architecture assessment

signalRules.signalPattern: Efficiency ratio >65% for two quarters with compliance cost >8% of non-interest expense
signalRules.recommendedAction: Launch efficiency improvement program with technology modernization and process automation assessment

signalRules.signalPattern: Detection latency >300ms OR APP scam losses >0.25% of RTP volume
signalRules.recommendedAction: Upgrade real-time transaction monitoring to sub-100ms with behavioral biometrics and confirmation of payee

signalRules.signalPattern: SMB primary bank share <35% OR deposit outflows >12% annually OR loan abandonment >60%
signalRules.recommendedAction: Develop SMB integrated banking platform with embedded payments and lending pre-qualification

signalRules.signalPattern: Charge-off rate >4.5% OR 30+ delinquency >3.2% for two months
signalRules.recommendedAction: Tighten underwriting criteria for near-prime segments and enhance collections strategy

signalRules.signalPattern: Advisory revenue decline >25% YoY OR MD compensation ratio >38% OR pitch-to-win <12%
signalRules.recommendedAction: Review sector coverage model and pitch database; evaluate MD compensation structure and headcount right-sizing

signalRules.signalPattern: Monthly burn >$15M OR CAC >$250 with deposit per account <$1,200 for two quarters
signalRules.recommendedAction: Launch path-to-profitability initiative with product mix optimization and CAC channel efficiency review

signalRules.signalPattern: Membership growth <1.5% OR average age >52 OR digital engagement <25%
signalRules.recommendedAction: Develop digital-first member acquisition strategy with Gen Z value proposition and fintech partnership assessment

signalRules.signalPattern: Brokered deposits >12% of total funding OR quarterly brokered growth >15%
signalRules.recommendedAction: Develop core deposit growth strategy and brokered deposit replacement plan

signalRules.signalPattern: Pull-through rate <55% for two months OR lock expiration rate >20%
signalRules.recommendedAction: Review lock desk pricing competitiveness and processor capacity constraints; evaluate rate float policies

personas.name: Chief Lending Officer (CLO)
personas.roleDescription: Responsible for all lending portfolios including commercial, consumer, mortgage, and specialty lending. Sets credit policy, manages underwriting standards, and drives loan growth while maintaining credit quality. Reports to CEO or President.
trustedEvidence: ABA Commercial Lending Committee survey data
RMA Annual Statement Studies (credit quality benchmarks)
Federal Reserve Senior Loan Officer Opinion Survey
S&P Global C&I and CRE market data
Mortgage Bankers Association origination cost studies
dislikedClaims: Assertions that credit decisions can be fully automated without human oversight
Generic ROI claims without loan-level analysis

personas.name: Branch Operations Manager
personas.roleDescription: Oversees retail branch network operations including staffing, customer experience, transaction processing, and branch P&L. Manages the transition from branch-centric to digital-first service delivery while maintaining community presence.
trustedEvidence: J.D. Power Retail Banking Satisfaction Study
Novantas branch analytics and cost benchmarks
Branch rationalization case studies from peer institutions
dislikedClaims: Claims that branches are completely obsolete
Digital-only solutions without human escalation paths
ROI calculations that ignore community banking relationships

personas.name: Digital Banking Product Owner
personas.roleDescription: Owns the digital banking product roadmap including mobile app, online banking, digital onboarding, and self-service capabilities. Balances feature velocity with regulatory compliance, security, and core system integration constraints.
trustedEvidence: App Annie / data.ai mobile banking rankings
dislikedClaims: Promises of 'launch in weeks' without core integration reality
Feature parity claims with neobanks without considering compliance overhead
Technology vendor claims of 'seamless integration' with legacy core systems

personas.name: Treasury Sales Officer / Head of Treasury Management
personas.roleDescription: Leads treasury management sales and product strategy for commercial clients. Responsible for deposit acquisition, earnings credit rate pricing, payment solutions, and liquidity services. Combines relationship management with product expertise.
trustedEvidence: Federal Reserve H.8 deposit data by type
dislikedClaims: Generic assertions about 'best-in-class' treasury platforms without API evidence
ROI claims that ignore switching costs for treasury clients
Disregard for earnings credit rate sensitivity in commercial relationships

personas.name: Mortgage Operations VP
personas.roleDescription: Oversees end-to-end mortgage origination operations including processing, underwriting, closing, and secondary market execution. Manages LOS, vendor relationships, compliance with TRID/RESPA, and cost-to-close optimization.
trustedEvidence: Mortgage Bankers Association cost and productivity studies
ICE Mortgage Technology Origination Insight Report
dislikedClaims: Assertions that mortgage can be fully automated to <10 days without regulatory friction
LOS migration promises without data migration risk assessment
Cost savings claims that exclude vendor change fees

personas.name: Payments Product Manager
personas.roleDescription: Owns card and payment product strategy including debit, credit, commercial cards, real-time payments (RTP/FedNow), and digital wallets. Balances interchange revenue, rewards economics, fraud prevention, and regulatory compliance.
dislikedClaims: Interchange revenue projections that ignore Durbin 2.0 legislative risk
Fraud prevention claims without latency specifications
Rewards program ROI without breakage and redemption analysis
personas.reportingLine: Head of Cards / Chief Product Officer

discoveryQuestions.questionText: How has your deposit beta trended over the last four quarters, and what is your target in the current rate environment?
discoveryQuestions.businessGoal: Assess deposit pricing strategy maturity and funding cost pressure

discoveryQuestions.questionText: What is your current cost-to-close and cycle time for mortgage originations, and how do those compare to your top three competitors?
discoveryQuestions.businessGoal: Identify mortgage operations efficiency gap and vendor constraint

discoveryQuestions.questionText: What percentage of your branch network is currently cash-flow positive, and how many lease renewals are you facing in the next 24 months?
discoveryQuestions.businessGoal: Assess branch rationalization urgency and lease obligation risk

discoveryQuestions.questionText: What is your current CRE concentration relative to risk-based capital, and how has your watch list trended in the last two quarters?
discoveryQuestions.businessGoal: Evaluate CRE credit risk and provisioning pressure

discoveryQuestions.questionText: How are you managing lease-end volume and residual value risk in your auto portfolio given current used vehicle price trends?
discoveryQuestions.businessGoal: Assess auto lease risk exposure and remarketing strategy

discoveryQuestions.questionText: What is your digital account opening abandonment rate, and where in the funnel do you see the highest dropout?
discoveryQuestions.businessGoal: Identify digital onboarding friction points and IDV vendor gaps

discoveryQuestions.questionText: How has your commercial operating deposit balance trended, and what ECR spread are you currently offering relative to Fed Funds?
discoveryQuestions.businessGoal: Evaluate treasury deposit retention risk and competitive pricing

discoveryQuestions.questionText: What is your current interchange yield trend, and how much of that revenue is being consumed by rewards program costs?
discoveryQuestions.businessGoal: Assess card portfolio profitability and legislative risk

discoveryQuestions.questionText: What is your commercial RFP win rate, and what are the top three reasons you lose deals to competitors?
discoveryQuestions.businessGoal: Identify commercial banking competitive gaps and decision speed issues

discoveryQuestions.questionText: What is the average age and AUM productivity of your private banking advisors, and what is your strategy for next-generation client engagement?
discoveryQuestions.businessGoal: Evaluate succession risk and next-gen platform readiness

discoveryQuestions.questionText: How many APIs does your core banking system support, and what is your average product time-to-market for new digital features?
discoveryQuestions.businessGoal: Assess core system modernization need and digital velocity constraints

discoveryQuestions.questionText: What is your efficiency ratio trend, and what percentage of non-interest expense is consumed by compliance and regulatory costs?
discoveryQuestions.businessGoal: Evaluate cost structure health and compliance burden

discoveryQuestions.questionText: What is your current fraud detection latency on real-time payment rails, and what percentage of APP scam losses are you absorbing?
discoveryQuestions.businessGoal: Assess real-time payment fraud risk and detection capability gap

discoveryQuestions.questionText: What percentage of your SMB clients name you as their primary bank, and how many financial providers do they typically use?
discoveryQuestions.businessGoal: Evaluate SMB relationship depth and competitive fragmentation

discoveryQuestions.questionText: How has your credit card portfolio yield trended, and what is your charge-off rate by FICO segment?
discoveryQuestions.businessGoal: Assess card portfolio profitability and underwriting quality

discoveryQuestions.questionText: What is your investment banking pitch-to-win ratio, and how has your MD compensation as a percentage of revenue trended?
discoveryQuestions.businessGoal: Evaluate deal pipeline efficiency and compensation structure sustainability

discoveryQuestions.questionText: What is your current customer acquisition cost per account, and what is your monthly burn rate relative to active account base?
discoveryQuestions.businessGoal: Assess digital bank unit economics and path to profitability

discoveryQuestions.questionText: What is your membership growth rate, average member age, and what percentage of your technology budget is allocated to digital?
discoveryQuestions.businessGoal: Evaluate credit union demographic risk and digital investment adequacy

discoveryQuestions.questionText: How are you preparing for Basel III endgame capital requirements, and what is your current CET1 ratio relative to your buffer target?
discoveryQuestions.businessGoal: Assess capital adequacy and regulatory preparation

discoveryQuestions.questionText: What is your current mortgage pipeline pull-through rate, and what percentage of locks are expiring before closing?
discoveryQuestions.businessGoal: Evaluate mortgage pipeline efficiency and pricing competitiveness

objections.objectionText: Our core banking vendor says they can add APIs next year—we should wait.
objections.recommendedResponse: API roadmaps from legacy core vendors typically take 18-36 months with limited endpoint coverage. The cost of waiting includes lost digital revenue, higher vendor lock-in, and competitive disadvantage. What is the penalty cost of delaying a product launch by 12 months?
supportingEvidence: Celent core banking vendor roadmap analysis

objections.objectionText: We already looked at mortgage automation and the ROI wasn't there.
objections.recommendedResponse: Many mortgage automation assessments underestimate the value of cycle time reduction on pull-through rates and the compounding effect of cost-per-loan at volume. Let's recalculate using your current pull-through and cost-to-close against 2024 benchmarks.

objections.objectionText: Our regulators won't let us reduce branches that fast.
objections.recommendedResponse: Regulators evaluate branch reductions against CRA performance and community impact, not arbitrary limits. Banks with strong CRA ratings have successfully consolidated 15-25% of networks. What does your CRA exam history show?

objections.objectionText: We're a relationship bank—automation would hurt our service model.
objections.recommendedResponse: Automation supports relationship banking by freeing RMs from administrative tasks and accelerating credit decisions. Top relationship banks achieve 3-5 day decision times while maintaining relationship quality. What percentage of RM time is spent on non-client-facing tasks?

objections.objectionText: Our depositors are sticky—we don't need to worry about beta.
objections.recommendedResponse: Deposit stickiness varies by segment and rate environment. Historical beta may not predict behavior in sustained high-rate environments. Brokered deposit growth and CD promotional rates are early warning signals. What is your deposit outflow rate in the last two rate cycles?

objections.objectionText: Real-time payments fraud is the customer's responsibility, not ours.
objections.recommendedResponse: While RTP settlement is irrevocable, regulatory and reputational risk increasingly places liability on banks for APP scams. UK Faster Payments experience shows banks absorbing 40-60% of losses. What is your current APP scam reimbursement policy?

objections.objectionText: We can't justify the technology spend as a community bank.
objections.recommendedResponse: Community banks are increasingly leveraging cloud-native, API-first platforms with subscription models that reduce upfront CapEx. Shared service models and consortium approaches reduce per-bank cost by 40-60%. What is your current technology spend as a percentage of assets?

objections.objectionText: Our current LOS vendor says they'll add AI features in their next release.
objections.recommendedResponse: Vendor AI roadmaps for legacy LOS typically deliver incremental features rather than transformative workflow automation. The opportunity cost of waiting is 12-18 months of elevated cost-to-close. Let's model the cost of delay against your current pipeline.

objections.objectionText: We're too small to benefit from treasury management platform upgrades.
objections.recommendedResponse: SMB treasury clients are increasingly demanding API integrations, real-time reporting, and self-service capabilities that legacy platforms can't provide. Losing 2-3 treasury clients to a competitor with modern capabilities can exceed $5M in deposit value. What is your average treasury client lifetime value?

objections.objectionText: Digital banks don't need branches, so our cost structure is already optimized.
objections.recommendedResponse: While digital banks avoid branch costs, CAC and unit economics often remain challenging. Top digital banks achieve profitability through deposit balance growth, interchange optimization, and lending product cross-sell. What is your CLV-to-CAC ratio and monthly burn per active account?

regulatoryFactors.name: Basel III Endgame (B3E) / Basel IV
regulatoryFactors.impactDescription: Proposed changes to standardized approach for credit risk, operational risk, and output floor. Estimated 10-20% increase in RWA for large banks. Community banks (<$100B) may see limited direct impact but indirect pressure through competitive dynamics.
regulatoryFactors.nonCompliancePenalty: Enforcement actions, growth restrictions, dividend limitations
regulatoryFactors.currentStatus: Proposed rule; comment period closed; implementation expected 2025-2028 phased
sources: Federal Reserve Basel III Notice of Proposed Rulemaking

regulatoryFactors.name: Dodd-Frank Act Stress Testing (DFAST/CCAR)
regulatoryFactors.impactDescription: Annual stress testing for banks >$100B. Includes quantitative and qualitative assessments. Proposed threshold changes may affect banks $100B-$250B.
regulatoryFactors.nonCompliancePenalty: MRA/MRIA, restriction on capital distributions, M&A limitations
regulatoryFactors.currentStatus: Active; thresholds under review per Economic Growth and Regulatory Relief Act

regulatoryFactors.name: Community Reinvestment Act (CRA) Modernization
regulatoryFactors.impactDescription: New CRA evaluation framework replacing old exam system. Retail and product test for large banks; community development test for all. Branch location decisions more scrutinized.
regulatoryFactors.nonCompliancePenalty: Downgraded rating, M&A application denial, business restriction

regulatoryFactors.name: TILA-RESPA Integrated Disclosure (TRID) / Regulation Z
regulatoryFactors.impactDescription: Mortgage disclosure requirements with strict timing and accuracy rules. TRID 2.0 updates expand coverage. Remediation costs for disclosure errors.
regulatoryFactors.nonCompliancePenalty: CFPB enforcement, restitution, civil money penalties up to $5,000/day
regulatoryFactors.currentStatus: Active; TRID 2.0 effective; ongoing CFPB examinations

regulatoryFactors.name: Bank Secrecy Act / Anti-Money Laundering (BSA/AML)
regulatoryFactors.impactDescription: Customer identification, transaction monitoring, SAR filing, beneficial ownership. AML Act of 2020 introduced corporate transparency requirements. Enhanced scrutiny on real estate and shell company transactions.
regulatoryFactors.nonCompliancePenalty: Consent orders, civil money penalties ($10M+), DOJ referral, charter revocation risk
regulatoryFactors.currentStatus: Active; AML Act implementation ongoing; beneficial ownership registry live 2024

regulatoryFactors.name: Current Expected Credit Loss (CECL)
regulatoryFactors.impactDescription: Forward-looking lifetime expected credit loss model replacing incurred loss. Higher provisioning volatility, especially for longer-duration portfolios (CRE, auto leases). Day 1 capital impact 5-15% for some banks.
regulatoryFactors.nonCompliancePenalty: Accounting restatement, SEC enforcement, auditor qualification
regulatoryFactors.currentStatus: Effective for all public companies; credit unions delayed to 2024-2025

regulatoryFactors.name: Durbin Amendment / Interchange Fee Regulation
regulatoryFactors.impactDescription: Caps debit interchange for banks >$10B. Proposed Durbin 2.0 would extend to credit interchange. Direct revenue impact 20-40% for affected card portfolios.
regulatoryFactors.currentStatus: Debit caps active; credit interchange legislation proposed but not enacted

regulatoryFactors.name: Fair Lending / ECOA and HMDA
regulatoryFactors.impactDescription: Prohibition on discrimination in lending. HMDA data collection and public disclosure. AI/ML underwriting models subject to disparate impact scrutiny.
regulatoryFactors.nonCompliancePenalty: DOJ investigation, consent decrees, restitution ($10M+), reputation damage
regulatoryFactors.currentStatus: Active; CFPB focus on AI in underwriting; HMDA data expansion 2024

regulatoryFactors.name: Section 1071 Small Business Lending Data Collection
regulatoryFactors.impactDescription: Requires collection and reporting of demographic data for SMB loan applications. Similar to HMDA for commercial lending. Data quality and system modification requirements.
regulatoryFactors.currentStatus: Final rule; phased implementation by lender size through 2025

regulatoryFactors.name: FedNow Service Participation Requirements
regulatoryFactors.impactDescription: Participation in FedNow instant payment rail requires compliance with operating rules, liquidity management, and fraud prevention standards. 24/7/365 operational readiness.
regulatoryFactors.currentStatus: Live July 2023; growing adoption; optional participation
sources: Federal Reserve FedNow Service Operating Rules

regulatoryFactors.name: FASB Fair Value Measurement (ASC 820)
regulatoryFactors.impactDescription: Affects valuation of illiquid assets, CRE loans, and securities portfolios. Increased disclosure requirements for Level 3 assets. CECL and fair value interplay on held-for-sale portfolios.
regulatoryFactors.currentStatus: Active; ongoing amendments to disclosure requirements

regulatoryFactors.name: NCUA Cybersecurity and IT Examination Framework
regulatoryFactors.impactDescription: Enhanced IT examination procedures for credit unions including cybersecurity, business continuity, and vendor management. CAT framework adoption.
regulatoryFactors.currentStatus: Active; NCUA ACET/CAT framework in use

buyingTriggers.triggerDescription: NIM declining >20bps with deposit beta >75% and brokered deposits growing >15% quarterly
Cost-to-close >$11,000 with pull-through rate <60% and cycle time >50 days
Board mandate to reduce efficiency ratio >5 points with >20% of branch leases renewing in 18 months
>$500M CRE maturing in 12 months with office vacancy >20% and watch list >10%
Residual losses >$600 per unit for two quarters with remarketing days-to-sell >50
Digital onboarding abandonment >65% with IDV failure >30% and competitor launch <3-minute onboarding
Top 10 treasury clients representing >$500M deposits terminating or reducing balances >20%
Durbin 2.0 legislative advancement with interchange yield already declining >7bps annually
Loss of 3+ major commercial relationships in one quarter with RFP win rate <25%
Senior advisor retirement wave (>20% in 18 months) with next-gen engagement <15%
Core banking contract renewal >$60M with >10% price increase and no API roadmap
Board sets efficiency ratio target <60% with current >68% and regulatory exam pending
APP scam losses >0.3% of RTP volume with >$2M quarterly losses and regulatory inquiry
SMB deposit outflows >15% with primary bank share <30% and fintech partnership discussions active
Monthly burn >$15M with <18 months cash runway and path to profitability >36 months

workedExamples.scenarioName: Regional Bank NIM Recovery Through Deposit Beta Management
workedExamples.scenarioDescription: $25B regional bank with deposit beta at 78% and NIM compressed to 2.85%. Brokered deposits at 14% of funding. Implementing deposit analytics platform and pricing optimization to reduce beta to 55%.
workedExamples.sourceAttribution: Based on FDIC deposit beta peer data and ALCO model sensitivity
workedExamples.sensitivityRange: $95M-$150M depending on rate trajectory and competitive response

workedExamples.scenarioDescription: $50B bank originating 45,000 mortgage loans annually. Current cost-to-close $10,800, cycle time 48 days, pull-through rate 62%. Implementing modern LOS with automated income/ asset verification and eClose.
workedExamples.calculation: ($10,800 - $6,500) * 45,000 + (48 - 28) * $85 * 45,000 = $193.5M + $76.5M = $270M annual value. Additional pull-through improvement from 62% to 72% = +4,500 loans * $2,500 margin = $11.25M. Total = $281.25M.
workedExamples.sourceAttribution: MBA 2024 cost benchmarks, ICE Mortgage Technology cycle time data
workedExamples.sensitivityRange: $220M-$340M depending on volume fluctuation and vendor implementation
risks: LOS migration risk and data quality

workedExamples.scenarioName: National Bank Real-Time Payment Fraud Prevention Investment
workedExamples.scenarioDescription: $200B national bank processing $15B annually on RTP/FedNow. Current fraud detection latency 450ms. APP scam losses at 0.28% of RTP volume ($42M annually). False positive rate 18% causing customer friction.
workedExamples.calculation: (0.004 * $15B) + (0.35 * $42M) + $5M + $3M = $60M + $14.7M + $5M + $3M = $82.7M annual value. Implementation cost $12M. Year 1 net = $70.7M.
workedExamples.sourceAttribution: UK Finance APP scam data scaled to US RTP volumes, Javelin fraud study benchmarks
workedExamples.sensitivityRange: $55M-$105M depending on scam volume trend and false positive accuracy
risks: Integration complexity with legacy auth systems

evidenceSources.coverageDescription: Aggregate financial data for all FDIC-insured institutions including NIM, efficiency ratio, deposit trends
Weekly aggregate balance sheet data for commercial banks including deposits, loans, securities
Industry-level mortgage origination cost, productivity, and profitability benchmarks

evidenceSources.sourceName: J.D. Power Retail Banking / Digital Banking Studies
evidenceSources.coverageDescription: Customer satisfaction rankings, digital adoption benchmarks, branch experience metrics

evidenceSources.sourceName: S&P Global Market Intelligence Bank Scorecards
evidenceSources.coverageDescription: Peer comparison analytics, credit quality scores, profitability benchmarking for 8,000+ banks
Card industry volume, interchange, fraud, and profitability data
Corporate treasury practices, bank relationship satisfaction, technology adoption
Commercial real estate vacancy, pricing, delinquency, and refinancing data

evidenceSources.sourceName: Experian / TransUnion / Equifax Credit Industry Reports
evidenceSources.coverageDescription: Auto finance, credit card, and consumer lending delinquency, originations, and performance data

evidenceSources.sourceName: Cerulli Associates Private Banking / Wealth Management
evidenceSources.coverageDescription: Advisor productivity, AUM trends, platform adoption, next-gen client data
Call report data for all federally insured credit unions including membership, assets, earnings
Digital bank funding, unit economics, CAC, and path-to-profitability analysis

competitorFactors.threatDescription: Scale advantages in technology spend, deposit beta management, and digital experience. Cross-selling 4-6 products per retail household vs. 2-3 for regional banks.
competitorFactors.differentiationOpportunity: Community relationship, local decision-making, personalized service
competitorFactors.marketShareTrend: Stable to growing in digital; declining in branch-dependent segments

competitorFactors.threatDescription: Superior digital onboarding (<3 minutes), no fee structures, and high NPS. Capturing younger demographics and fee-sensitive depositors.
competitorFactors.differentiationOpportunity: Full-service relationship, lending products, branch access when needed
competitorFactors.marketShareTrend: Growing deposit share; profitability challenges for some

competitorFactors.threatDescription: Lower mortgage cost-to-close through automation and direct-to-consumer model. Cycle times 20-30% shorter than traditional banks.
competitorFactors.differentiationOpportunity: Relationship-based servicing, portfolio retention, cross-sell to deposit clients
competitorFactors.marketShareTrend: Consolidating; market share volatile with rate cycles

competitorFactors.competitorName: Direct Lenders / Private Credit (Ares, Oaktree, Apollo)
competitorFactors.threatDescription: Faster credit decisions, less regulated, flexible structures. Taking share in middle-market lending and CRE.
competitorFactors.differentiationOpportunity: Lower cost of funds, deposit relationships, regulatory compliance trust
competitorFactors.marketShareTrend: Growing rapidly in private credit; approaching $1.7T market

competitorFactors.threatDescription: Embedded banking, expense management, and credit cards targeting venture-backed and tech SMBs.
competitorFactors.differentiationOpportunity: Full lending suite, treasury services, branch relationship for non-tech SMBs
competitorFactors.marketShareTrend: Growing in tech/SMB segments; limited penetration in traditional industries

competitorFactors.threatDescription: Collaborative technology investments and shared services reducing per-institution cost. Strong community affinity.
competitorFactors.differentiationOpportunity: N/A - collaborative rather than competitive for credit unions

competitorFactors.threatDescription: Wallet and payment integration threatening card top-of-wallet status. Limited direct banking but high customer engagement.
competitorFactors.differentiationOpportunity: Full banking relationship, lending, wealth management, regulatory trust
competitorFactors.marketShareTrend: Growing in payments; limited deposit/lending traction

overriddenComponents.overrideReason: This subpack contains only vertical-specialized additions. No master pack components were overridden. All master pack components (pains, KPIs, value drivers, formulas, benchmarks, signal rules, personas, etc.) remain valid and applicable to the banking vertical. Banking-specific components extend and specialize the master framework without conflict.
